ideas.title: Asynchronous, Fault-Tolerant CMA-ME via MapReduce-Inspired Architecture
ideas.description: This idea scales CMA-ME to thousands of parallel evaluations for extremely expensive functions by drawing an analogy from large-scale data processing. The analogous problem of processing petabytes of data across unreliable clusters was solved by the MapReduce model, which uses a master to distribute independent map tasks and aggregate (reduce) results. Adapted to CMA-ME, a central master node manages the archive and CMA-ES distribution, asynchronously dispatching evaluation 'map' tasks to a massive pool of workers (e.g., cloud instances, HPC nodes). Workers evaluate the expensive function and return fitness and gradient information. The master then applies a 'reduce' phase to robustly integrate these asynchronous, potentially out-of-order or failed results to update the search distribution and quality-diversity archive, enabling fault-tolerant, massively parallel optimization.

ideas.title: Dynamic Topological Archive for Hybrid Continuous-Discrete Domains
ideas.description: This research direction proposes a novel Quality-Diversity (QD) algorithm for hybrid or discrete domains that replaces the standard fixed-grid archive with a dynamic, topology-adaptive structure. The archive grows and reconfigures its cells or clusters based on the distribution and local connectivity of discovered solutions in the parameter space, rather than a pre-defined discretization. This allows the algorithm to efficiently map complex, irregular manifolds of high-performing behaviors in domains where a natural continuous embedding is absent or non-uniform. The synergy enables effective exploration and characterization of discrete design spaces—like graph structures, program syntax trees, or combinatorial choices—with the adaptive resolution and coverage typically only possible in continuous spaces.

ideas.title: Overfitting to one level is beneficial for transfer
ideas.description: Hypothesize that deep overfitting to a single, complex seed level can transfer well to similar levels.
ideas.sourceTitle: Illuminating Generalization in Deep Reinforcement Learning through Procedural Level Generation

ideas.title: Robust Inverse Design via Feasibility Manifold Learning and Probabilistic Tolerance Optimization
ideas.description: This approach learns a structured, low-dimensional manifold from the archive that represents the space of achievable system behaviors, explicitly modeling the uncertainty and multi-modality of the inverse mapping. Given a target behavior, the system does not seek a single exact solution but identifies the region of input space most likely to produce outcomes within a user-defined tolerance. It then performs a constrained optimization within this feasible region, balancing primary objective attainment with auxiliary goals like parameter simplicity or environmental robustness, yielding practical, realizable designs.

ideas.title: Apply CMA-ME to Protein Engineering via a High-Throughput Screening Analogy
ideas.description: This idea proposes evaluating CMA-ME on the complex, high-dimensional real-world problem of protein engineering, drawing an analogy from evolutionary biology's directed evolution. In directed evolution, large variant libraries are screened and iteratively refined to optimize protein function, effectively navigating a vast fitness landscape. The adapted solution is to frame protein design as a quality-diversity (QD) problem: CMA-ME will explore the sequence space, using an archive to maintain diverse functional 'phenotypes' (like stability ranges or expression levels) while directly optimizing a primary objective function (e.g., catalytic efficiency). This tests CMA-ME's ability to handle the intricate, constrained, and expensive-to-evaluate landscapes typical of real-world biomolecular design, providing a rigorous benchmark against traditional optimization methods used in the field.

ideas.title: Scale up: generate interconnected worlds or campaigns
ideas.description: Extend PCGRL to generate sequences of levels forming a coherent world or narrative campaign.
ideas.sourceTitle: PCGRL: Procedural Content Generation via Reinforcement Learning

ideas.title: Adaptive Filtering for CMA-ME Using Signal Processing Techniques
ideas.description: This idea challenges the assumption that CMA-ME requires deterministic or low-noise fitness evaluations by drawing an analogy from signal processing. In communications systems, adaptive filters like Kalman filters are used to recover clean signals from noisy channels by modeling both the signal dynamics and noise statistics. We adapt this solution by integrating an adaptive filtering layer into CMA-ME's evaluation pipeline. Each solution's fitness would be processed through a filter that estimates the underlying true fitness from noisy samples, with filter parameters (e.g., smoothing factors, confidence weights) dynamically adjusted based on the observed noise characteristics across the solution archive. This would allow CMA-ME to maintain reliable gradient estimates and archive management even in domains with highly stochastic or noisy fitness functions, effectively separating the exploration signal from the evaluation noise.

ideas.title: Relax assumption that more variation always helps
ideas.description: Study when and how PCG-induced variation harms learning or generalization.
ideas.sourceTitle: Increasing generality in machine learning through procedural content generation

ideas.title: Inverse: Derive PCG seeds from real data
ideas.description: Learn PCG parameters or rules from real-world observations to bootstrap simulation.
ideas.sourceTitle: Increasing generality in machine learning through procedural content generation

ideas.title: Conservative Bandits with Degradation-Aware Safeguards for Non-Replenishable Emitter Selection
ideas.description: This approach uses a constrained multi-armed bandit algorithm where each arm (emitter) is modeled with a finite, degrading capacity that diminishes with each activation. The system maintains a dual objective: maximizing information gain while minimizing the risk of rendering an emitter permanently inoperative. It incorporates a safety layer that predicts degradation based on activation history and material models, imposing a dynamic cost on selections that threaten long-term archive integrity. This ensures the archive's viability is preserved over an extended horizon, transforming a purely exploitative strategy into a sustainable stewardship policy.

ideas.title: Incorporate population-based training hyperparameter optimization
ideas.description: Merge PBT to co-evolve network weights and hyperparameters like learning rates simultaneously.
ideas.sourceTitle: AlphaStar: an evolutionary computation perspective

ideas.title: Inverse Design in Mixed Discrete-Continuous Spaces via Archive-Guided Search
ideas.description: This research direction develops methods to find input configurations—spanning both discrete and continuous parameters—that produce a user-specified target behavior in a complex system. It leverages a pre-computed archive of system behaviors not just as a lookup table, but as a structured search space to guide efficient optimization across heterogeneous parameter types. The core synergy is a unified framework that reasons about the interplay between discrete structural choices and continuous tuning, using the archive to inform proposals and prune invalid regions. This enables inverse design for previously intractable problems in fields like robotics (selecting actuator types and their control parameters) and materials science (choosing composite constituents and their processing conditions).

ideas.title: Inverse Design via Archive-Guided Compressed Sensing
ideas.description: This idea adapts the solution from compressed sensing in medical imaging to the inverse design problem. In compressed sensing MRI, a high-fidelity image is reconstructed from limited data by leveraging sparsity in a transform domain. Here, we posit that the space of input parameters that yield interesting or specified behaviors is 'sparse' in the high-dimensional design space. The archive of prior simulations or experiments serves as an implicit dictionary or basis set. The inverse problem—finding an input for a target behavior—is reformulated as finding a sparse linear or nonlinear combination of archive entries whose simulated output matches the target. By using L1-regularized optimization or matching pursuit techniques, we can efficiently solve for the critical parameters, using the archive to guide the search and dramatically reduce the number of new forward model evaluations required, scaling efficiently with the complexity of the behavior space.

ideas.title: Lightweight CMA-ME via Compressed Sensing for Fast, Low-Dimensional Illumination
ideas.description: This research seed proposes creating a simplified variant of CMA-ME (Covariance Matrix Adaptation MAP-Elites) for the rapid illumination of low-dimensional behavior spaces. The analogy is drawn from compressed sensing in signal processing, which reconstructs sparse signals from very few measurements. The adapted solution treats the behavior-characteristic map as a compressible signal. A lightweight CMA-ME variant would be designed to take a minimal set of randomized, information-rich evaluations in the low-dimensional space. Using a fast, sparse reconstruction optimization (e.g., based on l1-regularization), it would infer the complete archive, enabling extremely fast performance on problems where the behavior space is inherently low-dimensional and the underlying mapping is suitably structured, prioritizing evaluation speed over exhaustive coverage.

ideas.title: Adapting Homotopy Continuation Methods for Discrete Optimization
ideas.description: This idea challenges the assumption that hybrid solvers are limited to continuous parameter spaces by drawing an analogy from algebraic geometry. The analogous solution from numerical analysis is homotopy continuation, where discrete solution sets are found by tracking continuous paths from a solved system to a target system. The adapted research idea applies this framework to optimization in mixed or discrete domains. It proposes constructing a continuous homotopy embedding for the discrete problem, transforming the search into following a path in a continuous space. The existing hybrid solver's components are repurposed: gradient-based methods navigate the path, while discrete local search mechanisms manage branching or selection at singularities where the path splits, effectively allowing a continuous method to 'discover' discrete solutions.

ideas.title: Hierarchical CMA-ME with Multi-Resolution Protein Folding Analogy
ideas.description: This idea adapts a solution from computational biology—specifically, the multi-resolution hierarchical search strategies used in protein structure prediction (e.g., Rosetta)—to the context of Hierarchical CMA-ME for modular design spaces. In the analogous field, the search for a protein's native fold is made tractable by first exploring a coarse-grained conformational space of structural motifs before refining promising candidates at atomic detail. Adapted here, the CMA-ME algorithm is structured to first search a high-level space defined by the composition and coarse connections of modules, using a fast surrogate measure of solution quality. The archive of promising high-level designs from this search then seeds and constrains a subsequent lower-level search, where CMA-ME refines the detailed internal parameters of the constituent modules using a precise objective function. This hierarchical, multi-resolution approach efficiently scales exploration to match the natural duality of modular design spaces: the broad architectural choices and the deep parametric tuning within chosen architectures.

ideas.title: Combine CMA-ME with other QD archiving techniques
ideas.description: Explore hybrid archives that blend CMA-ME's adaptation with other diversity maintenance methods.
ideas.sourceTitle: Covariance matrix adaptation for the rapid illumination of behavior space

ideas.title: Apply Adaptive Optics' Wavefront Sensing to Archive Illumination
ideas.description: This idea adapts the solution from astronomical adaptive optics, where sparse wavefront sensing guides efficient correction of atmospheric distortion, to the problem of archive illumination. In adaptive optics, a system doesn't measure the entire wavefront continuously; it uses a guide star and a sensor to take key measurements, infers the complete distortion, and applies a correction. Analogously, for exploring a vast archive (e.g., of designs, strategies, or parameters), we can use a probabilistic surrogate model as a 'novelty/promisingness wavefront sensor.' This model is updated with a strategically chosen, sparse set of samples from the archive. A Bayesian optimization control loop then uses this model to infer the broader landscape of interesting regions and directs future sampling to 'correct' the exploration towards high-novelty, high-performance areas, dramatically improving sampling efficiency over random or grid-based methods.

ideas.title: Apply Bayesian optimization for archive illumination
ideas.description: Use probabilistic models to guide sampling towards promising and novel regions efficiently.
ideas.sourceTitle: Covariance matrix adaptation for the rapid illumination of behavior space

ideas.title: Scalable Hybrid Archive with Approximate Indexing and Localized Topology Refinement
ideas.description: This approach combines a scalable, static approximate nearest-neighbor index for efficient global querying with a lightweight dynamic layer that performs topology adaptation only in localized regions of the search space where novelty or improvement is being discovered. The system monitors archive modification density and triggers computationally cheap, rule-based splits or merges of archive cells in active regions, while leaving the vast majority of the archive indexed statically for performance. This ensures the adaptability benefits of a dynamic archive without the crippling computational cost of continuously rebuilding a global topological structure in high dimensions.

ideas.title: Asynchronous Decentralized CMA-ME with Lock-Free Archive and Emitter Scheduling
ideas.description: This system overcomes the synchronization bottleneck of parallel CMA-ME by decoupling evaluation from central updates. Multiple independent emitter processes asynchronously propose batches of solutions to a shared, lock-free archive, which aggregates results without global synchronization. A scheduler dynamically launches new emitters in promising or novel regions of behavior space based on archive statistics, and emitters can be initialized using gradient information or surrogate models from nearby archive entries to warm-start search. This design enables sustained, full utilization of thousands of parallel workers for expensive black-box functions while maintaining the quality-diversity search properties of CMA-ME.

ideas.title: Use multi-armed bandits for emitter selection
ideas.description: Apply bandit algorithms to dynamically choose which archive cell's emitter to activate.
ideas.sourceTitle: Covariance matrix adaptation for the rapid illumination of behavior space

ideas.description: Scale CMA-ME to thousands of parallel evaluations for extremely expensive functions.

ideas.description: Use a dynamic, growing, or topology-adaptive archive instead of a fixed grid.
